sections.a_description: Every indie project dies of something. We diagnose what's killing yours — before it flatlines.

a_image.alt: Dark laboratory workbench with petri dishes containing glowing digital organisms and code fragments, microscope in the center, cinematic side lighting

sections.a_description: 12 criteria. Rate each 1–3. If anything scores a 1 — that's a knockout.
sections.summary_incomplete: Rate all criteria to see your diagnosis.
sections.verdict_knockout: KNOCKOUT — your project has a sickness. Full stop.
sections.verdict_solid: Solid foundation — verify the yellow areas before launch.
sections.verdict_weak: Too many unknowns. Step back — the project isn't ready.
sections.verdict_stop: Stop. This isn't a project yet — it's an idea.

items.question: I can build and launch this in one week.
items.a_description: If it takes longer, the scope is too big. Simplify or postpone.

items.question: Anyone understands the value without me explaining it.
items.a_description: Test on 3 people. If you're explaining for more than 30 seconds, rework the pitch.

items.question: I can fund this myself, no investor needed.
items.a_description: Calculate your runway — time and money. If you need funding, you're playing a different game.

items.question: I know exactly who this is for.
items.a_description: Prove it with a specific name or community. "Everyone over 30" is not an answer.

items.question: People actually need this and would pay for it today.
items.a_description: Ask real people — don't assume. Politeness is not validation.

items.question: This will make money with a clear pricing model.
items.a_description: Set a pricing model before launch. One-time sales are harder than recurring.

items.question: The market has low competition and organic discoverability.
items.a_description: Check Google, App Store, Product Hunt. Without an organic channel, you need a budget.

items.question: This project is morally and ethically sound.
items.a_description: Projects without integrity drag and hurt. If you hesitate, the answer is no.

items.question: I am not telling myself things I know aren't true.
items.a_description: Every project has spots where you're lying to yourself. Write them down. If you can't disprove them with evidence, they're still lies — just better packaged.

items.question: I have verified my core assumptions with real evidence.
items.a_description: "People will want this." "We'll make money." "I can do it alone." Maybe. But until it's verified, it's just a story you're telling yourself.

items.question: I can name three weaknesses without starting each with "but."
items.a_description: Write down the three biggest weaknesses of the project. Read them again. If each one starts with "but" — those are excuses.

items.question: What remains when you subtract lies, assumptions, and bullshit?
items.a_description: This is the real state of the project — no sugar-coating. If nothing remains, the idea isn't bad. It's just not ready yet.

seo.title: Sickness Labs — Diagnose What's Killing Your Indie Project
seo.description: Identify the diseases that kill side projects, startups, and indie products. From founder syndrome to feature creep — diagnose your project before it's too late.
